speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: So shall it be. Your Grace shall stay behind

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text:
So strongly guarded.
                 
                To
                 
                Arthur
                .
               Cousin, look not sad.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Thy grandam loves thee, and thy uncle will

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: As dear be to thee as thy father was.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O, this will make my mother die with grief!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Cousin, away for England! Haste before,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And ere our coining see thou shake the bags

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Of hoarding abbots; imprisoned angels

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Set at liberty. The fat ribs of peace

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Must by the hungry now be fed upon.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Use our commission in his utmost force.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Bell, book, and candle shall not drive me back

speaker: BASTARD
text: When gold and silver becks me to come on.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I leave your Highness.—Grandam, I will pray,

speaker: BASTARD
text: If ever I remember to be holy,

speaker: BASTARD
text: For your fair safety. So I kiss your hand.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Farewell, gentle cousin.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Coz, farewell.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Come hither, little kinsman. Hark, a word.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Come hither, Hubert.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: O, my gentle Hubert,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: We owe thee much. Within this wall of flesh

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: There is a soul counts thee her creditor,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And with advantage means to pay thy love.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And, my good friend, thy voluntary oath

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Lives in this bosom dearly cherishèd.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Give me thy hand. I had a thing to say,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But I will fit it with some better tune.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: By heaven, Hubert, I am almost ashamed

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To say what good respect I have of thee.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I am much bounden to your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Good friend, thou hast no cause to say so yet,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But thou shalt have. And, creep time ne’er so slow,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Yet it shall come for me to do thee good.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I had a thing to say—but let it go.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The sun is in the heaven, and the proud day,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Attended with the pleasures of the world,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Is all too wanton and too full of gauds

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To give me audience. If the midnight bell

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Did with his iron tongue and brazen mouth

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Sound on into the drowsy race of night;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: If this same were a churchyard where we stand,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And thou possessèd with a thousand wrongs;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Or if that surly spirit, melancholy,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Had baked thy blood and made it heavy, thick,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Which else runs tickling up and down the veins,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Making that idiot, laughter, keep men’s eyes

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And strain their cheeks to idle merriment,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A passion hateful to my purposes;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Or if that thou couldst see me without eyes,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Hear me without thine ears, and make reply

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Without a tongue, using conceit alone,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Without eyes, ears, and harmful sound of words;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Then, in despite of brooded watchful day,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I would into thy bosom pour my thoughts.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But, ah, I will not. Yet I love thee well,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And by my troth I think thou lov’st me well.

speaker: HUBERT
text: So well that what you bid me undertake,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Though that my death were adjunct to my act,

speaker: HUBERT
text: By heaven, I would do it.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Do not I know thou wouldst?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Good Hubert, Hubert, Hubert, throw thine eye

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: On yon young boy. I’ll tell thee what, my friend,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: He is a very serpent in my way,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And wheresoe’er this foot of mine doth tread,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: He lies before me. Dost thou understand me?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Thou art his keeper.

speaker: HUBERT
text: And I’ll keep him so

speaker: HUBERT
text: That he shall not offend your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Death.

speaker: HUBERT
text: My lord?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A grave.

speaker: HUBERT
text: He shall not live.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Enough.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I could be merry now. Hubert, I love thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Well, I’ll not say what I intend for thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text:
Remember.
                 
                He
                 
                turns
                 
                to
                 
                Queen
                 
                Eleanor
                .
               Madam, fare you well.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I’ll send those powers o’er to your Majesty.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: My blessing go with thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: For England, cousin, go.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Hubert shall be your man, attend on you

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: With all true duty.—On toward Calais, ho!
